title: Staining of single-cell suspensions for scMEP mass cytometry analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5f274","text":"Staining of single-cell suspensions for scMEP analysis by mass cytometry (CyTOF). This protocol includes 1) surface staining for 30min at RT, 2) PFA-fixation for 10min at RT, 3) MeOH-based permeabilization for 10min on ice 4) intracellular staining for 1h at RT and 5) DNA intercalation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"T4QQ6","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"sMdxS","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"xtWL2","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":649008,"guid":"4266763C341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Cell-ID™ Intercalator-Rh—500 µM","mol_weight":0,"sku":" 201103A","url":"https://www.fluidigm.com/reagents/proteomics/201103a-cellid-intercalator-rh--500micrometer","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":291,"name":"Fluidigm","link":"https://www.fluidigm.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":649009,"guid":"4266768D341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"EQ™ Four Element Calibration Beads","mol_weight":0,"sku":" 201078","url":"https://www.fluidigm.com/reagents/proteomics/201078-eq-four-element-calibration-beads--100ml","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":291,"name":"Fluidigm","link":"https://www.fluidigm.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Prepare single cell suspensions in FACS tubes as described in the following protocol: Preparation of single-cell suspensions for scMEP mass cytometry analysis by Felix J Hartmann
Perform palladium-based barcoding as described in the following protocol: (still needs to be written)

Prepare surface antibody mastermix. Note: Antibodies were titrated for 3 million cells, if more cells are to be used, scale antibody amounts accordingly (i.e. use 2x antibodies for up to 6 million cells). Combine all surface antibodies and supplement volume to a total of 50 µL with Cell Staining Medium (CSM: PBS + 0.5% BSA + 0.02% sodium azide). If antibody volume succeeds 50 µL , no CSM addition is needed.
Add antibody mastermix to cells and mix carefully by pipetting up and down, followed by a gentle vortex. Incubate for 00:30:00 at Room temperature .
Add 2 mL of CSM, mix carefully and centrifuge 600 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes, Reduce to 300g when working with live cells .
Aspirate supernatant and dissolve pellet by flicking tube or vortexing.

Dilute 16% PFA to 1.6% in PBS. PFA from Electron Microscopy Sciences has been shown to work well. Opened 16% PFA can be stored airtight and light-protected (e.g. in black falcon tube) for up to two weeks. Diluted 1.6% PFA should be discarded after use. Reagents: 16% Paraformaldehyde (PFA) Fisher Scientific Catalog #50-980-487

Add 1 mL of freshly diluted 1.6% PFA to loosened cells, mix well and incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature to fix antibodies onto cells. Also complete this section when working with pre-fixed cells.
Add 3 mL, or whatever your vessel allows of Cell Staining Medium (CSM) and centrifuge 600 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Aspirate supernatant and loosen cell pellet by flicking and vortexing.

Note: MeOH based permeabilization has been found to work well with intracellular metabolic antibodies although most are suspected to work with saponin-based approaches as well. While vortexing cells, drop-wise add 1 mL of pre-cooled ( 4 °C ) MeOH and immmediately transfer cells On ice to incubate for 00:10:00 .
Following incubation, add 3 mL, or whatever your vessel holds of CSM and centrifuge 600 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Aspirate supernatant and loosen cell pellet.
Repeat washing by adding 3 mL, or whatever your vessel holds of CSM and centrifuge 600 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes . Aspirate supernatant and loosen cell pellet.

Prepare intracellular antibody mastermix. Note: Antibodies were titrated for 3 million cells, if more cells are to be used, scale antibody amounts accordingly (i.e. use 2x antibodies for up to 6 million cells). Combine all intracellular antibodies and supplement volume to a total of 50 µL with CSM. If antibody volume succeeds 50 µL , no CSM addition is needed.

Add antibody mastermix to cells and mix carefully by pipetting up and down, followed by a gentle vortex. Incubate for 01:00:00 at Room temperature . Flick tube carefully after approximately 00:30:00 to ensure proper mixing of antibodies and cells.
Add 2 mL of CSM, mix carefully and centrifuge 600 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes . Aspirate supernatant and loosen pellet.

Prepare intercalation solution: 1.6% PFA in PBS + 0.5 micromolar (µM) of Intercalator-Rh. Note: Iridium-based DNA intercalators can be used alternatively, however they do tend to spill into adjacent platinum channels which are often used here for live/dead discrimination as well as for antibody channels. Rhodium DNA intercalation is compatible with palladium-based barcoding. When using for the first time, aliquot Rh-intercalator and store at -20 °C until use Rh-intercalator can be thawed and re-frozen multiple times Cell-ID™ Intercalator-Rh—500 µM Fluidigm Catalog # 201103A
Add 1 mL of intercalation solution for each 10 million cells and incubate either 00:20:00 at Room temperature or Overnight at 4 °C .
First CSM wash step: Add 3 mL, or whatever the vessel holds of CSM to the cells and centrifuge 600 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes . Aspirate supernatant and loosen cell pellet.
Second ddH2O wash step: Add 3 mL, or whatever the vessel holds of ddH2O to the cells and centrifuge 600 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes . Aspirate supernatant and loosen cell pellet.
Third ddH20 wash step: Add 3 mL, or whatever the vessel holds of ddH2O to the cells and centrifuge 600 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes . Aspirate supernatant and loosen cell pellet.
Dilute EQ beads 1:10 in ddH2O and add 1 mL of diluted beads per 1 million cells. EQ™ Four Element Calibration Beads Fluidigm Catalog # 201078
Filter cells through cell strainer and acquire on CyTOF
creator: Felix J Hartmann
